claim: A finance worker at a multinational firm in Hong Kong was tricked into transferring HK$200 million (~US$25.6M) over 15 transactions in February 2024 after a video call with a deepfake CFO and other deepfaked colleagues.
quote: A finance worker at a multinational firm was tricked into paying out $25 million to fraudsters using deepfake technology to pose as the company's chief financial officer in a video conference call, according to Hong Kong police.

claim: The victim of the HK$200M deepfake video-call fraud was British engineering and design firm Arup; Arup confirmed fake voices and images were used and notified Hong Kong police in January 2024.
quote: Arup, a British multinational design and engineering company known for designing the Sydney Opera House... told CNN on Friday that it notified Hong Kong police in January about the fraud incident, and confirmed that fake voices and images were used.

claim: The Arup deepfake attack used existing online conference footage to synthesize the CFO and colleagues; the lone real participant was the targeted finance worker.
quote: Hong Kong police determined that the perpetrators developed AI generated deepfakes of the finance worker's CFO and colleagues by leveraging existing video and audio files of these individuals from online conferences and virtual company meetings.

claim: WPP CEO Mark Read was the target of a May 2024 deepfake Microsoft Teams attack using a WhatsApp account with a public photo, AI-cloned voice, and YouTube footage; the scam failed.
quote: Hackers attempted to defraud WPP executives by using deepfake and AI voice cloning technology to impersonate CEO Mark Read.

claim: The WPP attack used a fake WhatsApp account, a Teams meeting, AI voice cloning of Mark Read, and chat-function impersonation to solicit money and personal details from another executive.
quote: The scammers then arranged a Microsoft Teams meeting, impersonating Read both visually and vocally... fraudsters utilised the chat function to further impersonate Read, trying to solicit money and personal details from the targeted executive.

claim: On 23 July 2024 KnowBe4 disclosed it had hired a North Korean fake IT worker who used a stolen US identity, an AI-enhanced photo, and passed four video interviews; malware loaded on the issued laptop within minutes of receipt.
quote: We sent them their Mac workstation, and the moment it was received, it immediately started to load malware... HR conducted four video conference based interviews on separate occasions, confirming the individual matched the photo provided on their application.

claim: Mandiant and FBI investigation confirmed the KnowBe4 North Korean operative used deepfake technology and a VPN; KnowBe4 was not breached because the new-hire account had limited permissions.
quote: An investigation conducted with Mandiant and the FBI showed that the threat actor used deepfake technology to obtain the job and a VPN to manipulate their location.

claim: In December 2024 the DOJ indicted 14 DPRK nationals for an IT-worker scheme that generated over $88 million for North Korea's weapons programs over six years.
quote: North Korea's fake IT worker scam hauled in $88M

claim: Microsoft tracks the DPRK IT-worker cluster as Jasper Sleet (overlaps with 'Famous Chollima'); operators use AI-generated synthetic identities, deepfakes for interviews, and laptop farms inside the US.
quote: Jasper Sleet: North Korean remote IT workers' evolving tactics to infiltrate organizations

claim: FBI PSA July 2025 warns DPRK IT workers use real-time deepfake video and stolen identities to gain remote employment at US firms; by November 2025 DOJ reported 136 victim companies and seized $15M in related crypto.
quote: North Korean IT Worker Threats to U.S. Businesses

claim: DeepFaceLive — a Windows real-time face-swap tool for streaming and video calls — has 30.8k GitHub stars and was archived by its owner on 13 Nov 2024.
quote: Real-time face swap for PC streaming or video calls. This repository was archived by the owner on Nov 13, 2024. It is now read-only.

claim: Deep-Live-Cam — successor-style 'real-time face swap and one-click video deepfake with only a single image' — has 92.4k GitHub stars and is actively maintained, dominating the live face-swap category.
quote: real time face swap and one-click video deepfake with only a single image

claim: The original Faceswap project ('Deepfakes Software For All') sits at ~55.1k GitHub stars and 13.4k forks, the foundational training-based deepfake toolkit.
Roop, the popular one-click face-swap repo, reached ~30.5k stars before being archived in March 2026 with the author citing concerns about 'second-order effects' of the software.
quote: When I originally built this project, I approached it primarily as a technical experiment in generative media. Not long after releasing it, my view of the broader second-order effects around software in this category changed.

claim: In March 2025 a Singapore-based finance director at a multinational was deepfaked into wiring US$499,000 during a fully synthetic Zoom call featuring multiple fake executives posing as the UK-based CFO.
quote: A finance director at a multinational firm in Singapore authorized a US$499,000 payment during what appeared to be a Zoom call with the company's senior leadership... the entire video call was fabricated using deepfake technology.

claim: Resemble AI's Q1 2025 report tallies more than $200 million in deepfake-enabled fraud losses in the first four months of 2025, with video the dominant modality (46%) followed by images (32%) and audio (22%).
quote: Deepfake-Enabled Fraud Has Already Caused $200 Million in Financial Losses in 2025

claim: Sumsub's 2025–26 Identity Fraud Report finds deepfake fraud up roughly 700% globally Q1 2024–Q1 2025, deepfakes now ~7% of all fraud attempts, and 76% of fraud attempts occur after KYC verification.
quote: A staggering 700% global increase in deepfake fraud occurred between Q1 2024 and Q1 2025... Deepfakes now represent 7% of global fraudulent activity.

claim: Reality Defender's 2025 video-detection model upgrade claims an 8% improvement in balanced accuracy over the prior model with broader generator coverage; Gartner named it a 2025 market leader in deepfake detection.
quote: Reality Defender's new video model delivers an 8% improvement in balanced accuracy, along with support for a greater number of closed- and open-source deepfake generation platforms.

claim: Microsoft's Video Authenticator (released 2020) returns a real-time confidence score by detecting deepfake blending boundaries; Microsoft has not published a current accuracy figure for the tool.
quote: Video Authenticator can analyze a still photo or video to provide a percentage chance, or confidence score, that the media is artificially manipulated.
